rationale: Round-3 fixes are coherent and grounded. (1) ADR-0037 §8 carve-out (Bead 3 step 4 + ADR Fitness) is well-scoped: it grants durability ONLY to the audit of an APPLIED refutation, on the active-clearance-vs-passive-footgun distinction, with operator authorization named. The general posture stays intact — empty reason still clears, panel freely removable for a no-refutation bead, no signing/hashing, adversary-who-never-refutes not targeted. Amending §8 (not silently contradicting it) is correct since the durable-obligation moves the audit onto bd metadata and touches §8's out-of-repo/no-resistance fence. ADR-0023 is handled: ADR Fitness adds 3 refinements + the execution/internal/bead Impacted-Domains addition; ADR-0037 Domain(s) already cover execution. (2) Testing Strategy names every NEW case with real seams I verified: cross-run panel-removed retry 7(B)(iv), pending-write-fail abort 7(B)(iii), TestGetMetadata 7(B)(vi), erasure-survival+TestMergeMetadata_FailClosedOnReadError, AC12 dedup. closeBeadFn/completeMergeMetadataFn/setupPanelGateRepo/readStubMergeExecutor.completeCalled/execCommand all exist; the MergeMetadata read-swallow bug is real. (3) mindspec validate spec passes (exit 0) with execution declared.
findings: ADR-0037 §8 amendment is a genuinely narrow, well-justified carve-out — durability of an APPLIED-refutation audit only; every other §8 property (empty-reason clears, panel removable for no-refutation beads, no signing/hashing, non-adversary target) explicitly re-affirmed. Amending §8 rather than re-affirming is the right call because the durable-obligation protocol relocates the audit to bd metadata and hardens MergeMetadata, which brushes §8's 'do not move state out of repo / add resistance' fence.

ADR-0023 execution-domain reach is handled end to end: Impacted Domains adds execution/internal/bead with a good-reason note (verified owned at execution/OWNERSHIP.yaml:3); ADR Fitness ADR-0023 section documents obligation-before-close, fail-open metadata consult, and fail-closed MergeMetadata as strengthenings within ADR-0023's spirit; no new ADR needed since ADR-0037 Domain(s) already include execution.
mindspec validate spec 114 => 'all checks passed', exit 0 — the execution Impacted-Domains addition satisfies the complete-time divergence gate as claimed (O3 mechanism confirmed).

All NEW test cases are named and land on existing seams: cross-run panel-removed retry that REFUSES on second run via the fail-open obligation override (7(B)(iv), asserted through closeBeadFn spy + readStubMergeExecutor.completeCalled); pending-marker-write-fails->abort pre-close (7(B)(iii)); TestGetMetadata + TestMergeMetadata_FailClosedOnReadError in bdcli_test (7(B)(vi)); erasure-survival (7(B)(v)); AC12 (slot,round) dedup extended into the audit path (7(A)(vii)). Seams verified present: closeBeadFn (complete.go:29), completeMergeMetadataFn (complete.go:42), setupPanelGateRepo/readStubMergeExecutor/completeCalled (panel_gate_e2e_test.go), execCommand (bdcli.go:24).

The described MergeMetadata bug is real in the current source: 'if err == nil' swallows a bd-show read error, then replace-writes from an empty merged map, erasing existing keys. The fail-closed fix (return the read/parse error; still merge from a clean empty read) is correct and the caller taxonomy (already-fatal / warn / ignore) is accurately characterized.

writePanelAuditMetadata confirmed best-effort (warn-and-continue) and only writes on the skip-env and abandoned branches — corroborating the plan's item-7 correction that the enforcement.panel_gate:false config toggle writes NO audit; the durable-vs-best-effort asymmetry is correctly grounded solely in a non-empty AppliedRefutations set, not any 'non-merge exit' claim.

Minor, non-blocking: the label 'anti-tamper carve-out' is slightly stronger than what is delivered (an audit-durability guarantee, not tamper-resistance), but the amendment text itself disambiguates precisely ('a party that never records a refutation is not the target'), so no wording change is required.
